content:
4 min read
Just now

--

In recent times, there has been a significant rise in people using LLMs to solve questions in coding contests. Almost every leaderboard you see after the contest ends -> whether its LeetCode, CodeForces, CodeChef or any other coding platform, almost all of the top 20 to 30 people have just copy-pasted the code thrown out by the LLM.
Here is the sad part:
Most of the profiles you see in these leaderboards, almost 90% of them are Indians.
If you see the code replay of the solutions that they wrote after the contest (which is a feature in LeetCode), you can clearly see how suddenly their text editor is changed from a blank text editor to a fully correct written code with a perfect syntax. Some of these accounts don’t even bother to remove the comments from the code that their LLM gave.
If you see the number of questions they have solved in the profiles of these type, they all have somewhere around 50–60 questions only.
This isn’t only for contests. Same goes with Online Assessments. You can hardly find anybody in today’s time who doesn’t cheat in OA’s.
Some of these profiles from the June 28, 2026 weekly contest from LeetCode:

This guy Aryan Chaudhary even had the audacity to post about this on his LinkedIn, although he removed commenting on his posts for obvious reasons.

India has one of the world’s largest digital markets, approaching roughly 1 billion active internet users, and with the rise of AI models, we have access to one of the strongest tools in the world.
But as the famous dialogue from Spider Man goes: “With great power comes great responsibly”.
Indians seem to violate that till oblivion.
All these people seem to destroy the image of India. Whether its civic sense at public places , whether its opening unnecessary PR’s to open source repositores(no wonder why in open source also Indians have a very bad reputation), or whether these type of things, due to a few handpicked people , the whole country’s reputation gets damaged.
Don’t get me wrong. Indians are one of the smartest people in the world. Majority of tech companies today are led by people from Indian origin.
What I think in my opinion is that, we are still living in a scarcity mode. We have one of the world’s youngest populations, but with that comes competition. This competition has turned significantly into a unhealthy one for almost all of today’s youth. You see somebody of your age doing almost everything perfectly of what you can only dream of, and you start living in a sense of stress and anxiety. Looking at other people around you doing absolutely better than you, you start to worry. Everybody has to get a job and start earning as India has one the lowest per capita GDP in the world. So people will do everything they have just to feed their stomach.
And to please everybody around you, you start taking shortcuts. As in our example, cheating just to brag to fellow friends or people online about your rating and ranking.
Is doing stuff like this helping you in any freaking way in your life?
Absolutely Not.
You can do whatever you want to do. Everybody has free will. But when the actual time comes when you have to actually use your brain to figure out everything, you will suffer undoubtedly. You will deeply regret not doing these stuff and just doing the hard things earlier.
So to anybody reading this:
Please focus on doing the hard things starting from now. As everything compounds over time exponentially. As written in Atomic Habits:
If you just improve 1% each day, after 365 days, you will be 37 times better than where you were before. This combined with consistency is a deadly combo which can beat almost anybody.
Peace out. ✌️
